waves.description: Contract Freeze / Scaffold - Define data classes and protocols for parallel execution

work_packets.title: Add parallel execution data classes to domain/models.py
acceptance_criteria: ParallelConfig with max_concurrency (default 3), max_concurrency_cap, effective_limit()
PacketResult with packet_id, wave_id, exit_code, stdout, stderr, duration_seconds
WaveResult with wave_id, packet_results, all_succeeded, failed_packets property
ExecutionPlanResult with wave_results, total_duration, is_success() method
work_packets.builder_prompt: Add the following dataclasses to domain/models.py: ParallelConfig (with effective_limit() method using os.cpu_count()), PacketResult, WaveResult, ExecutionPlanResult. Ensure proper imports for dataclass, typing, os.

work_packets.title: Add executor protocols to domain/protocols.py
acceptance_criteria: PacketExecutor ABC with execute_packet abstract method
WaveExecutor ABC with execute_wave abstract method
work_packets.builder_prompt: Add to domain/protocols.py: PacketExecutor (ABC) with async execute_packet(packet, wave_id, cancel_event) -> PacketResult; WaveExecutor (ABC) with async execute_wave(wave, wave_id, config, cancel_event) -> WaveResult. Import WorkPacket from models.

work_packets.title: Create test scaffold for parallel execution
acceptance_criteria: Test files created in tests/ directory for parallel execution
Tests stubbed for: concurrency limiting, fail-fast behavior, cancellation
work_packets.builder_prompt: Create tests/test_parallel_execution.py with stubbed test methods: test_concurrency_limiting, test_fail_fast_behavior, test_cancellation_handler. Use pytest and pytest-asyncio.

waves.description: Core Implementation - PacketExecutor and WaveExecutor with asyncio concurrency

work_packets.title: Implement PacketExecutor with subprocess execution
work_packets.builder_prompt: Create src/spec_orch/services/packet_executor.py implementing PacketExecutor protocol. Use asyncio.create_subprocess_exec to run 'codex exec' commands. Track timing with time.perf_counter(). Handle cancel_event check during execution. Return PacketResult with exit_code, stdout, stderr.

work_packets.title: Implement WaveExecutor with semaphore-based concurrency
acceptance_criteria: WaveExecutor implements protocol from protocols.py
Uses asyncio.Semaphore for concurrency limiting
effective_limit() caps at CPU cores
Uses asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=False for fail-fast
First exception cancels remaining packets
Returns WaveResult with all packet results
work_packets.builder_prompt: Create src/spec_orch/services/wave_executor.py implementing WaveExecutor. Use asyncio.Semaphore with config.effective_limit(). Execute packets concurrently using asyncio.gather (no return_exceptions) to achieve fail-fast. On any exception, cancel remaining and propagate. Return WaveResult with all_succeeded = all(r.exit_code == 0).

waves.description: Integration - Run controller integration, cancellation, and logging

work_packets.title: Integrate parallel execution into run controller
acceptance_criteria: Waves execute sequentially
Packets within wave execute in parallel
Failed wave aborts entire execution (no subsequent waves)
Returns ExecutionPlanResult with timing
work_packets.builder_prompt: Modify run_controller.py or create new execution flow that: 1) Iterates waves sequentially, 2) For each wave calls WaveExecutor.execute_wave() with ParallelConfig, 3) On wave failure (not all_succeeded), abort remaining waves and return early, 4) Collect all WaveResults into ExecutionPlanResult with total_duration.

work_packets.title: Implement SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGKILL cancellation handling
acceptance_criteria: Ctrl-C (SIGINT) sets cancel_event
Sends SIGTERM to all running subprocesses
Waits 5s, then SIGKILL if still running
Aborts remaining waves
work_packets.builder_prompt: Create src/spec_orch/services/process_manager.py with: ProcessManager class that tracks running subprocesses, handles SIGINT to set cancel_event, send_sigterm_to_all(), wait_then_sigkill(timeout=5), and cleanup(). Integrate with PacketExecutor to register processes.

work_packets.title: Add structured JSON logging with wave/packet correlation
acceptance_criteria: All log messages include wave_id and packet_id fields
Events: wave_started, wave_completed, wave_failed, packet_started, packet_completed, packet_failed, execution_started, execution_completed, execution_cancelled
Structured JSON output
work_packets.builder_prompt: Extend activity_logger.py to add logging methods for each event type (wave_started, wave_completed, etc.) that include wave_id and packet_id in the log record. Use event_formatter.py to format as structured JSON with required fields.

waves.description: Integration / QA - Unit tests and verification

work_packets.title: Write unit tests for concurrency, fail-fast, and cancellation
acceptance_criteria: test_concurrency_limiting: Verify max N packets run concurrently
test_fail_fast_behavior: First failure cancels remaining in wave
test_cancellation_handler: SIGINT triggers SIGTERM then SIGKILL flow
All tests pass
work_packets.builder_prompt: Implement the test scaffold in tests/test_parallel_execution.py: 1) test_concurrency_limiting - mock PacketExecutor, track concurrent executions, verify max concurrency, 2) test_fail_fast_behavior - mock slow executor that raises on first, verify remaining cancelled, 3) test_cancellation_handler - verify ProcessManager sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL sequence.

work_packets.title: Integration verification and CLI test
acceptance_criteria: CLI accepts --max-concurrency flag
End-to-end execution with parallel waves produces correct result structure
Logging output includes wave_id and packet_id
work_packets.builder_prompt: Add --max-concurrency argument to cli.py (default 3). Pass to ParallelConfig in execution. Test end-to-end run with a simple spec to verify ExecutionPlanResult returned with correct structure and timing.
